description: Tier-2 & Tier-3 Freshers-Only Hiring Platform - A marketplace for non-elite college students with AI onboarding, paid-only jobs, and direct chat

userStories.title: Initialize Next.js project with Supabase and Tailwind
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded with Next.js (App Router), Supabase client, and Tailwind CSS so all subsequent stories have a working foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 14+ project created with App Router (app/ directory)
Tailwind CSS installed and configured with a warm, approachable color palette (avoid corporate blue)
Supabase client library installed (@supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr)
Environment variables set up for NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (with .env.local.example)
Supabase client utility created at lib/supabase/client.ts (browser) and lib/supabase/server.ts (server)
Basic layout.tsx with global styles applied
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create database schema — users, profiles, colleges, skills tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core database tables so auth, onboarding, and college gating can store data.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates `profiles` table extending Supabase auth.users with columns: id (FK to auth.users), role ('candidate' | 'employer'), onboarding_status ('pending' | 'in_progress' | 'completed'), eligibility ('eligible' | 'ineligible'), full_name, created_at

SQL migration creates `candidate_profiles` table with columns: id, user_id (FK), work_vibe, role_intent, skill_identity, job_preference, strengths (text[]), dealbreakers (text[]), availability, college_id (FK), resume_url, github_url, linkedin_url, portfolio_url, projects (jsonb), created_at, updated_at

SQL migration creates `employer_profiles` table with columns: id, user_id (FK), fresher_type, hiring_intent, company_context, role_category, top_skills (text[]), hiring_philosophy, candidate_type_pref, work_style, city, compensation_type, created_at, updated_at
SQL migration creates `colleges` table with columns: id, name, tier (1 | 2 | 3), state, type ('IIT' | 'NIT' | 'IIIT' | 'BITS' | 'IISc' | 'Private' | 'State' | 'Deemed' | 'Other'), created_at
SQL migration creates `skills` table with columns: id, name, category
Index on colleges.name for fast partial text search
RLS policies: users can read/update their own profile rows only
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create database schema — companies, jobs, applications, messages tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the job posting, application, and messaging tables for the employer and hiring pipeline features.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration creates `companies` table: id, employer_id (FK to profiles), name, logo_url, industry, vibe_description, created_at

SQL migration creates `jobs` table: id, company_id (FK), employer_id (FK), title, description, role_type ('internship' | 'full_time' | 'freelance'), compensation (integer, must be > 0), location, work_style ('remote' | 'in_office' | 'hybrid'), skill_tags (text[]), deadline (date), status ('active' | 'filled' | 'expired'), created_at, updated_at

SQL migration creates `applications` table: id, candidate_id (FK), job_id (FK), status ('applied' | 'shortlisted' | 'interview' | 'offer' | 'hired' | 'rejected'), created_at, updated_at. Unique constraint on (candidate_id, job_id)
SQL migration creates `messages` table: id, sender_id (FK), receiver_id (FK), application_id (FK), content (text), is_read (boolean default false), created_at
CHECK constraint on jobs.compensation > 0
RLS policies: employers see their own jobs/applications; candidates see their own applications; message access restricted to sender/receiver
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed colleges master list and skills list
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the colleges table populated with Tier-1/2/3 institutions and the skills table with predefined options.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed script (supabase/seed.sql or scripts/seed-colleges.ts) populates all 23 IITs as Tier-1
All 31 NITs seeded as Tier-1
All 26 IIITs seeded as Tier-1
BITS Pilani (all campuses), IISc Bangalore seeded as Tier-1
At least 500 Tier-2 and Tier-3 colleges seeded with correct state and type
Skills table seeded with: Python, Java, Web Development, UI/UX Design, Content Writing, Influencer Marketing, Sales/Outreach, Data Analysis, Operations, Product Thinking, Communication
Seed script is idempotent (can be run multiple times without duplicates)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build landing page with role selection
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a landing page that explains the platform so I can decide to sign up as a candidate or employer.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page at route / with hero section and platform tagline about fair hiring for Tier-2/3 freshers
Two prominent CTA buttons: 'Get Started as Student' (links to /signup?role=candidate) and 'Hire Freshers' (links to /signup?role=employer)
Brief section with 3 value props: Tier-2/3 only, no unpaid roles, AI vibe matching
Mobile responsive layout
Warm, approachable design (not corporate blue)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build signup form with email and password
userStories.description: As a user (candidate or employer), I want to sign up with my name, email, and password.
acceptanceCriteria: Signup page at /signup with fields: Full Name, Email, Password
Role is read from URL query param (?role=candidate or ?role=employer) and stored
Email validated for format; password requires minimum 8 characters with inline validation
On submit, calls Supabase Auth signUp with email confirmation enabled
On success, creates a row in `profiles` table with role and onboarding_status='pending'
Redirects to /verify-otp page on success
Shows inline error for duplicate email or other auth errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build OTP verification screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to verify my email with a 6-digit OTP so my account is trusted.
acceptanceCriteria: OTP page at /verify-otp with 6 separate digit input fields that auto-focus to the next field
On submit, calls Supabase Auth verifyOtp
60-second cooldown timer before 'Resend OTP' button becomes active
Invalid/expired OTP shows red inline error text
On successful verification, redirects candidate to /onboarding/candidate and employer to /onboarding/employer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build reusable chat UI component for AI onboarding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable full-screen chat component that renders AI messages, typing animation, and tap-able pill/bubble options.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatUI component at components/chat/ChatUI.tsx
Renders message bubbles: AI messages on the left, user selections on the right
AI messages appear with a typing animation (dots, then text reveal)
Supports rendering tap-able pill/bubble options (single-select and multi-select modes)
When user taps a pill, it appears as their response bubble and triggers an onSelect callback
Multi-select mode shows a 'Continue' button after selecting the required number of options
Smooth auto-scroll to latest message
Full-screen layout, mobile-first, warm gradient background
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build LLM integration for onboarding chat
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a server-side API route that sends the questionnaire context to an LLM and returns conversational messages with structured option data.
acceptanceCriteria: API route at app/api/chat/route.ts using Next.js Route Handlers
Accepts POST with: role (candidate/employer), currentSection (number), previousAnswers (object)
Sends a system prompt to the LLM with the full question bank for the given role and section
System prompt instructs the LLM to respond in warm, casual, dating-app style tone
LLM response is parsed to extract: message text, options array (label + value), and select mode (single/multi)
Returns JSON: { message: string, options: [{label, value}], selectMode: 'single' | 'multi', maxSelections?: number }
Handles Claude API or OpenAI API (configurable via env var)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build candidate onboarding flow — Sections 0-3 (Vibe, Intent, Skills, Preferences)
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to answer the first 4 sections of the AI vibe check so the platform understands my personality and preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate onboarding page at /onboarding/candidate using the ChatUI component
Section 0 (Warm-up): AI asks about work vibe, presents 4 pill options (structured corporate / startup chaos / creative+flexible / I'm open)
Section 1 (Role Intent): AI asks what they're here for, 4 options (internship / full-time / freelance / exploring)
Section 2 (Skill Identity): AI asks 7-days-laptop question, 6 options (code / design / sell / analyze / manage / figuring it out)
Section 3 (Job Preferences): AI asks work style, 4 options (remote / in-office / hybrid / doesn't matter)
Each answer is stored in component state and persisted to candidate_profiles via server action after each section
Progress is saved — refreshing the page resumes from the last completed section
Calls the /api/chat endpoint for each section's AI message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build candidate onboarding flow — Sections 4-6 (Strengths, Dealbreakers, Availability)
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to answer sections about my strengths, dealbreakers, and availability.
acceptanceCriteria: Continues the /onboarding/candidate page from Section 4

Section 4 (Strengths): AI asks to pick exactly 2 strengths — multi-select pills from 6 options (fast learner / strong comms / technical skills / leadership / creative thinking / hardworking). Continue button appears after 2 selected.
Section 5 (Dealbreakers): AI asks what NOT to show — multi-select pills from 5 options (unpaid internships / sales roles / too corporate / too competitive / unrelated roles)
Section 6 (Availability): AI asks when they can start — 4 single-select options (immediately / 1 month / 3 months / browsing)
All answers persisted to candidate_profiles after each section
After Section 6, flow transitions to Section 7 (college gate)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build candidate onboarding — Section 7 (Tier-1 College Gate)
userStories.description: As the platform, I need to verify the candidate's college and block Tier-1 students.
acceptanceCriteria: After Section 6, AI asks 'which college are you from?'
Searchable dropdown appears in the chat, querying the colleges table with debounced partial text search (server-side query)
Dropdown shows college name and tier badge

If user selects a Tier-1 college: chat stops, polite fairness screen appears with message: 'Hey — this platform is only for Tier-2/Tier-3 freshers, so we can keep opportunities fair. You're already a rockstar, and we know you'll find something great elsewhere!'
Account flagged as ineligible in profiles table (eligibility='ineligible')
Ineligible users are redirected to a /blocked page on any subsequent login attempt
If Tier-2 or Tier-3 selected: college_id saved to candidate_profiles, flow proceeds to Section 8
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build candidate onboarding — Section 8 (Resume upload & portfolio)
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to upload my resume and optionally add portfolio links after passing the college gate.
acceptanceCriteria: After passing college gate, upload screen appears within the chat flow
Resume upload: mandatory, PDF only, max 5MB, stored in Supabase Storage bucket 'resumes/'
Optional text inputs: GitHub URL, LinkedIn URL, Portfolio URL
Optional: up to 3 project entries (title + short description) stored as JSONB
All data saved to candidate_profiles (resume_url, github_url, linkedin_url, portfolio_url, projects)
On completion, AI says 'I'm ready! Let's find your match' and a 'Go to Jobs' button redirects to /jobs
Profile onboarding_status updated to 'completed'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add incomplete onboarding guard middleware
userStories.description: As the platform, I need to redirect users with incomplete onboarding back to the vibe check on every login.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js middleware checks authenticated users' onboarding_status from profiles table
If onboarding_status is 'pending' or 'in_progress': redirect to /onboarding/candidate or /onboarding/employer based on role
If eligibility is 'ineligible': redirect to /blocked
Protected routes: /jobs, /dashboard, /chat — all require completed onboarding
Public routes: /, /signup, /verify-otp, /onboarding/*, /blocked — no redirect
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build employer onboarding flow — Sections 0-4 (Vibe, Intent, Context, Role, Skills)
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to answer the first 5 sections of the hiring persona chat.
acceptanceCriteria: Employer onboarding page at /onboarding/employer using the ChatUI component
Section 0 (Warm-up): 5 options (hungry learner / job-ready / creative builder / dependable executor / just someone solid)
Section 1 (Hiring Intent): 5 options (internship / full-time / freelance / bulk / exploring)
Section 2 (Company Context): 5 options (early-stage startup / growing business / corporate / agency / non-profit)
Section 3 (Role Category): 8 options (tech / marketing / sales / design / data / operations / product / other)
Section 4 (Skills): Multi-select top 3 from predefined skills list + 'Other' free text input
All answers persisted to employer_profiles after each section
Calls /api/chat with role='employer' for each section
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build employer onboarding flow — Sections 5-8 (Philosophy, Type, Work Style, Compensation)
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to complete the remaining onboarding sections about hiring philosophy and compensation.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 5 (Philosophy): 5 options (skills>college / attitude>exp / communication / speed / loyalty)
Section 6 (Candidate Type): 5 options (needs mentorship / independent / fast learner / structured / creative)
Section 7 (Work Style): work mode pills (remote / in-office / hybrid / flexible) + city searchable dropdown
Section 8 (Compensation): 4 options (stipend / CTC / freelance payout / performance+base). Shows 'Unpaid roles are not allowed on this platform' info message.
All answers persisted to employer_profiles
On completion, onboarding_status updated to 'completed', redirect to /onboarding/employer/company-profile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build employer company profile setup page
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to set up my company profile with name, logo, industry, and vibe description.
acceptanceCriteria: Company profile page at /onboarding/employer/company-profile
Form fields: Company Name (text), Logo (image upload), Industry (dropdown), Company Vibe description (textarea)
Logo upload stored in Supabase Storage bucket 'logos/'
Data saved to companies table linked to the employer's user_id
On submit, redirects to /dashboard/employer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build job posting creation form
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to create a paid job/internship listing.
acceptanceCriteria: Job posting page at /dashboard/employer/post-job

Form fields: Title, Description (rich text or textarea), Role Type (internship/full_time/freelance dropdown), Compensation in INR (number input), Location (text), Work Style (remote/in_office/hybrid), Skill Tags (multi-select from skills table), Application Deadline (date picker)
If compensation is 0 or empty: Publish button is disabled with tooltip 'Why we don't allow unpaid roles'
Must select at least 1 skill tag — validation error if none selected
Server action checks for duplicate job title by same employer within 24 hours — shows error if duplicate found
On success, job saved to jobs table with status 'active' and redirects to /dashboard/employer
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build job discovery feed for candidates
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to browse available jobs in a card-based feed.
acceptanceCriteria: Job feed page at /jobs showing job cards in a responsive grid
Each card shows: Role Title, Company Name, Company Logo, Compensation (₹), Role Type badge, Location, Work Style badge
Jobs listed in reverse chronological order (newest first)
Only shows jobs with status 'active' and deadline >= today
Paginated — 20 jobs per page with 'Load More' or pagination controls
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add filters to job feed
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to filter jobs by role type, work style, and skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter bar at top of /jobs page with dropdowns: Role Type (All/Internship/Full-time/Freelance), Work Style (All/Remote/In-office/Hybrid), Skill Tags (multi-select)
Filters update the job list without full page reload (client-side filtering or URL params with server query)
Active filters are visually indicated and clearable
Empty state message when no jobs match filters
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build job detail page
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to view the full details of a job posting.
acceptanceCriteria: Job detail page at /jobs/[id]
Shows: Title, Company Name + Logo, Full Description, Role Type, Compensation, Location, Work Style, Skill Tags, Deadline
Company vibe description shown in a sidebar or section
Quick Apply button (same behavior as on card — see US-022)
Back link to /jobs feed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Quick Apply functionality
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to apply to a job with one tap.
acceptanceCriteria: Quick Apply button on job cards and job detail page
If candidate has resume_url in candidate_profiles: clicking creates an application record with status 'applied' immediately
If no resume on file: clicking opens a PDF upload modal — after upload, application is submitted
Server action creates row in applications table with candidate_id, job_id, status='applied'
Duplicate prevention: if application already exists for this candidate+job, button shows 'Applied' (disabled state)
Success toast: 'Application submitted!'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build candidate application tracker dashboard
userStories.description: As a candidate, I want to see the status of all my applications.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate dashboard at /dashboard/candidate
Lists all applications with: Job Title, Company Name, Application Date, Current Status (applied/shortlisted/interview/offer/hired/rejected)
Status shown as colored badge for each pipeline stage
Sorted by most recent application first
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build employer application inbox
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to see all candidates who applied to my jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Employer dashboard at /dashboard/employer with application inbox
Applications grouped by job posting (accordion or tabs)
Each applicant row shows: Candidate Name, College Name, Key Skills (from onboarding strengths), Application Date, Current Status
One-click 'View Resume' button opens the candidate's PDF in a new tab
Sorted by most recent application first within each job group
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build hiring pipeline status controls
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to move candidates through pipeline stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Each applicant row in the inbox has a status dropdown: Applied / Shortlisted / Interview / Offer / Hired
Changing the dropdown updates the application status via server action immediately
Status change is reflected on the candidate's dashboard in real-time (or on next page load)
Visual confirmation of status change (toast or inline animation)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build direct chat — message data layer and API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need server actions for sending/receiving messages and Supabase Realtime subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Server action: sendMessage(senderId, receiverId, applicationId, content) — inserts into messages table
Server action: getMessages(applicationId) — returns messages for an application, cursor-based pagination (latest 50 first)
Server action: markAsRead(messageId) — sets is_read=true
Supabase Realtime subscription config for the messages table (INSERT events filtered by receiver_id)
Only the employer can send the first message (enforced in sendMessage — checks if any prior message exists from employer for this application)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build direct chat UI
userStories.description: As an employer or candidate, I want a chat interface to communicate in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat page at /chat/[applicationId]
Message list with sender-aligned bubbles (own messages right, other's left)
Text input with send button at the bottom
Real-time message updates via Supabase Realtime subscription (new messages appear without refresh)
If candidate tries to send first message and no employer message exists, show disabled input with hint 'Waiting for recruiter to start the conversation'
Messages marked as read when chat is opened
Auto-scroll to latest message on load and on new message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Open Chat button to employer inbox
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to open a chat with a candidate directly from the application inbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Each applicant row in the employer inbox has an 'Open Chat' button
Clicking navigates to /chat/[applicationId]
If no messages exist yet, the chat opens and the employer can send the first message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add in-app notification badge for unread messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a badge when I have unread messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation bar shows a chat/messages icon with a red badge count of unread messages
Badge count updates in real-time via Supabase Realtime subscription on messages table (filtered by receiver_id, is_read=false)
Clicking the icon navigates to a chat list page (/chat) showing all conversations
Chat list shows: other party's name, last message preview, unread count per conversation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build hire completion flow with confetti
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to mark a candidate as hired and celebrate the moment.
acceptanceCriteria: When employer changes applicant status to 'Hired' (from Offer stage): confirmation dialog appears 'Mark [Name] as Hired?'
On confirm: application status set to 'hired', job status changed to 'filled'
All other applications for the same job get status 'rejected' automatically
Candidate sees a confirmation prompt on their dashboard to accept the hire
Confetti animation plays on both employer and candidate screens (use canvas-confetti library)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add vibe feedback form after hire
userStories.description: As an employer, I want to optionally leave feedback after hiring a candidate.
acceptanceCriteria: After confetti animation, a modal appears with optional 'Vibe Feedback' form
Form has: 1-5 star rating and a short text field (max 280 chars)
Skip button to dismiss without feedback
Feedback saved to a `hire_feedback` table (application_id FK, rating, comment, created_at)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build shared navigation bar with role-based links
userStories.description: As a user, I want a consistent navigation bar that shows the right links based on my role.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared Navbar component in layout used across all authenticated pages
Candidate nav: Jobs Feed, My Applications, Chat, Profile
Employer nav: Dashboard (inbox), Post Job, Chat, Company Profile
Logo/brand name links to / (landing) if logged out, or /jobs (candidate) or /dashboard/employer (employer) if logged in
Logout button that calls Supabase auth signOut and redirects to /
Mobile: hamburger menu or bottom tab bar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
